4_implementation_depth (20).sphere_processing (8): 4/8 - Uses IcoSphereRef normals + asSpherical and grid_sample with a normalized grid, which matches the intended pipeline at a high level, but the ERP->grid normalization looks inconsistent (theta/phi scaling to [-1,1] is likely wrong), and the export uses border padding without clearly addressing longitude wrap-around. Several critical spherical ops (upsample/downsample mappings) appear risky/heuristic and the model code is truncated mid-implementation, so correctness can’t be validated end-to-end.
4_implementation_depth (20).model_architecture (7): 2/7 - Contains scaffolding for MLP and (down/up)sampling blocks and imports attention/position-encoding modules, but the provided model implementation is incomplete/truncated (e.g., NearestUpsample cut mid-line) and does not demonstrate a full U-shaped encoder-decoder Transformer with spherical local self-attention wiring, skip connections, and final heads.
4_implementation_depth (20).code_quality (5): 2/5 - Some modularization exists (loss, evaluator, export utilities), but there are clear robustness issues: truncated/incomplete files, likely bugs (e.g., CenterDownsample indexing assumes a specific layout), questionable numerical choices (BerHu delta uses max(diff).item()), inconsistent CLI typing (bool flags as int), and lack of explicit shape/device handling in export (grid/sample tensors not moved to device, align_corners not specified).

eval_report.comment: Partial - core parts exist but significant issues.

def get_rotation_matrices(rphitheta: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
    r, p, t = rphitheta.T
    p = (90-p) / 180 * np.pi
    t = t / 180 * np.pi
    zeros, ones = np.zeros_like(t), np.ones_like(t)
